text.title: Backup and Recovery for Solo VPS Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `Backup and Recovery for Solo VPS` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Backup MUST follow 3-2-1: 3 copies, 2 different media, 1 offsite. Anything less is a single point of failure.
rule.rationale: Datacenter fire and accidental rm both happen; 3-2-1 covers both. Anything less leaves a coverage gap.

rule.statement: PostgreSQL dumps MUST use custom format (-Fc) and be verified with `pg_restore --list` immediately after creation. Unverified dumps are rejected.
rule.rationale: pg_dump can exit 0 but produce truncated output if disk fills mid-write. --list reads the catalog without restoring data and fails loudly on corruption.

rule.statement: Restic repository password MUST live in a password manager AND in a local /root/.restic-password (chmod 600), never inside the restic repository itself.
rule.rationale: If the password is inside the backup, you cannot recover after a total server loss — which is the primary disaster scenario.

rule.statement: Restic retention MUST be `--keep-daily 7 --keep-weekly 4 --keep-monthly 6` with `--prune` in the same command as `forget`. Separate prune runs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Running forget without prune marks but does not reclaim; running prune separately doubles cron complexity. Combined keeps retention always current.

rule.statement: Restore drill MUST run monthly to a scratch host with `restic restore latest` + `pg_restore` + smoke check. Untested backups are not backups.
rule.rationale: A backup that has never been restored is a hope. Monthly drills make RTO real.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume cycles and produce false confidence.
